params.text:
Recording & reporting

Communication doesn't end when a conversation does. What you record and hand over keeps the person safe and their care consistent.

  • Write facts, not opinions — what you saw, heard and did.
  • Record promptly and sign/date every entry.
  • Report changes, concerns and refusals to the right person straight away.
  • Keep information confidential and share only on a need-to-know basis.

Key takeaway: good communication is two-way, adapted to the individual, and always followed by clear, factual recording. When you're ready, continue to the assessment to earn your certificate.
